title: Understanding Pagination
description: Test your understanding of Spring Data pagination.
question: A client requests GET /api/tasks?page=2&size=10. The database has 47 tasks total. What will the response's 'content' array contain?
options: Tasks 21-30 (page is zero-indexed, so page=2 is the third page)
Tasks 21-47 (all remaining tasks from page 2 onwards)
explanation: Spring Data pagination is zero-indexed, so page=0 is the first page, page=1 is the second, and page=2 is the third. With size=10 and page=2, the response returns tasks 21-30 (the third group of 10). The total of 47 tasks means there are 5 pages total (10+10+10+10+7).
